description: Clarify which data points (N=10^6, 10^9, 10^10, 10^11, 10^12) are measured, and which (e.g., for higher N) are extrapolations. The 5-point logarithmic fit (density = 31.5 + 4.47*log₁₀(N), R² = 0.9984) uses all five measured values: 57.98 at 10^6, 72.06 at 10^9, 76.55 at 10^10, 80.75 at 10^11, 84.58 at 10^12. The predicted density values for larger N (e.g., 10^15) are purely extrapolations.
action_taken: Clarify which data points (N=10^6, 10^9, 10^10, 10^11, 10^12) are measured, and which (e.g., for higher N) are extrapolations. The 5-point logarithmic fit (density = 31.5 + 4.47*log₁₀(N), R² = 0.9984) uses all five measured values: 57.98 at 10^6, 72.06 at 10^9, 76.55 at 10^10, 80.75 at 10^11, 84.58 at 10^12. The predicted density values for larger N (e.g., 10^15) are purely extrapolations.
